flashcards.back: A positive whole number greater than 1 that has more than two factors. (e.g., 4, 6, 8, 9, 10, ...).

flashcards.front: What is the smallest composite number?

flashcards.back: No. Many odd numbers are composite, such as 9, 15, 21, 25, 27, and 33.

flashcards.front: Why are 0 and 1 neither prime nor composite?
flashcards.back: A prime or composite number must be greater than 1. 1 has only one positive factor (itself), and 0 has infinitely many factors.

flashcards.front: What is the difference in factors between primes and composites?
flashcards.back: Primes have exactly two positive factors (1 and itself). Composites have three or more positive factors.
